options.text: I want to eat healthier this week. Can you suggest some quick dinner recipes that are nutritious?

I'm trying to eat healthier this week but only have 30 minutes max for dinner prep on weeknights. Can you suggest 5 nutritious dinner ideas that use common ingredients and include prep time estimates? I have a full kitchen and eat most foods except shellfish.

responses.response_weak: Here are some healthy dinner ideas: grilled chicken with vegetables, salmon with rice, a big salad with protein, stir-fry with tofu, and quinoa bowls. These are all nutritious options that you can prepare in different ways depending on your preferences. You could also try soups, wraps, or grain bowls for variety.
responses.response_medium:
Here are some quick, nutritious dinner ideas to help you eat healthier this week:

For protein-focused meals, try baked chicken breast with roasted vegetables, pan-seared salmon with a side salad, or turkey lettuce wraps with avocado. These are all high in protein and relatively simple to prepare.

For plant-based options, consider a chickpea and vegetable curry, black bean tacos with fresh salsa, or a lentil soup with crusty bread. These are budget-friendly and packed with fiber and nutrients.

To keep things quick, batch-cook your grains and proteins on Sunday so weeknight assembly takes just 10-15 minutes. Pre-washed salad greens and pre-cut vegetables are also great time-savers.
responses.response_strong:
## 5 Healthy Weeknight Dinners (30 Minutes or Less)

### 1. Sheet Pan Chicken and Vegetables — 25 min
- **Ingredients:** chicken thighs, broccoli, bell peppers, olive oil, garlic powder, paprika
- Cut everything into similar-sized pieces, toss with oil and seasoning, roast at 425°F
- **Why it works:** One pan, minimal prep, minimal cleanup

### 2. Black Bean Sweet Potato Tacos — 20 min
- **Ingredients:** sweet potato (diced small), canned black beans, whole wheat tortillas, avocado, lime
- Microwave sweet potato 8 min, warm beans with cumin and chili powder, assemble
- **Why it works:** No meat to cook, hearty and filling

### 3. Lemon Garlic Salmon with Green Beans — 18 min
- **Ingredients:** salmon fillets, green beans, lemon, garlic, butter or olive oil
- Pan-sear salmon 4 min per side, steam green beans in the same pan with a lid
- **Why it works:** Fast-cooking protein, one-pan method

### 4. Turkey Veggie Stir-Fry — 15 min
- **Ingredients:** ground turkey, frozen stir-fry vegetables, soy sauce, ginger, rice or cauliflower rice
- Brown turkey, add frozen veggies and sauce, serve over microwave rice
- **Why it works:** Frozen vegetables make this the fastest option

### 5. Chickpea Spinach Curry — 22 min
- **Ingredients:** canned chickpeas, canned diced tomatoes, fresh or frozen spinach, curry powder, rice or naan
- Simmer chickpeas, tomatoes, and curry powder 15 min, stir in spinach at the end
- **Why it works:** Entirely from pantry and freezer staples

**Note:** All recipes serve 2-3 people and use ingredients found at any standard grocery store. None contain shellfish.

feedback.what_happened: Compare the three responses side by side. The weak prompt produces a generic grocery list of ideas — 'grilled chicken with vegetables' tells you almost nothing you didn't already know. The medium prompt gets better general advice with some useful categories, but no specific timing, ingredient lists, or consideration of real-world constraints. The strong prompt specifies the 30-minute limit, requests 5 ideas with prep times, and mentions the shellfish restriction — so the AI delivers a complete weekly plan with exact cook times, ingredient lists, and practical notes for each recipe.
feedback.principle: When you tell the AI your real constraints — time limits, dietary needs, how many ideas you want — it stops giving you a vague brainstorm and starts giving you a usable plan.
feedback.tip: Include your top 2-3 constraints (time, dietary needs, skill level) and specify the format you want (like 'include prep times and ingredients'). This turns generic suggestions into a plan you can actually follow.
